title: Badge: Good Borrower
resultGreatDescription: You made {{score}} responsible borrowing decisions out of {{total}} challenges!
skillsDescription: You learned to return items on time, replace damaged items, and handle borrowed things with care!
wisdomDescription: These habits help you build trust and maintain good relationships!
resultKeepDescription: You made {{score}} responsible borrowing decisions out of {{total}} challenges.
resultKeepTip: Remember: return items on time, keep them safe, and treat borrowed things with care.

challenges.title: Book Responsibility
challenges.question: Your friend lent you a storybook last week. What should you do?

options.text: Return it clean and on time

feedback.correct: Excellent! Returning borrowed items on time builds trust!
feedback.wrong: Always return what you borrow to show respect and responsibility.

challenges.title: Pencil Care
challenges.question: You borrowed a pencil from your classmate. It broke while using. What now?

options.text: Replace it with a new pencil

feedback.correct: Perfect! Good borrowers replace damaged items.
feedback.wrong: If you damage something borrowed, replace it.

challenges.title: Phone Return
challenges.question: Your cousin let you use their phone. How should you return it?

options.text: Clean it and return with thanks

feedback.correct: Amazing! Returning items in good condition shows trustworthiness.
feedback.wrong: Return borrowed items in the same or better condition.

challenges.title: Umbrella Etiquette
challenges.question: Your neighbor lent you an umbrella. When should you return it?

options.text: As soon as possible, dried and clean

feedback.wrong: Return borrowed items quickly so others can use them.

challenges.title: Toy Handling
challenges.question: Your sibling lent you their favorite toy. How do you handle it?

options.text: Take extra care and return it safely
Play rough - it is just a toy

feedback.correct: Wonderful! Careful handling makes you a trusted borrower.
feedback.wrong: Treat borrowed items even better than your own.
